**Iran** seized a 'floating armoury' vessel in the Gulf of Oman, ratcheting up tension as Trump publicly says his patience with Tehran is running out.
The **SpaceX IPO** is set to lock in Musk's control via a Mars-linked pay deal at a $1.75 trillion valuation - investors appear willing to accept near-zero governance rights.
The **BOJ** is expected to raise rates to 1.0% in June and hike again later in the year - Alphabet's $3.6bn yen bond issue, the largest ever by a foreign firm, looks well-timed.
**Richard Socher's** new $650m startup aims to build an AI that researches and improves itself indefinitely - the self-improving AI arms race is now attracting serious venture capital.
**Scottish councils** paid out over 2 million pounds in pothole damage claims over five years - a small but pointed reminder of chronic infrastructure underinvestment.

**Hiscox** shares hit record highs after Canada's Intact Financial Corp was reported to be exploring a takeover bid for the London-listed insurer.
**IS second-in-command** Abu-Bilal al-Minuki was killed in a joint US-Nigerian operation, Trump announced - a rare counter-terrorism win in West Africa.
**Iran** told Washington it is ready for more nuclear talks, as Trump said a 20-year suspension of Tehran's nuclear programme would be sufficient - the negotiating window appears open but narrow.
**Mali's junta**, backed by Russian mercenaries, launched airstrikes against a rebel coalition of Islamist and Tuareg fighters as control of the country's north deteriorates rapidly.
**Tesco CEO Ken Murphy** took home **10.8 million pounds** last year - up over 1 million pounds - as the supermarket hit its highest UK market share in a decade.

**Iraq** formally swore in new PM Ali al-Zaidi, who pledged economic reforms in a country still structurally dependent on oil revenues above $70 a barrel to balance its budget.
**Alex Saab**, a Venezuelan official and close Maduro ally accused of laundering billions in sanctions-busting deals, was deported to the US to face federal charges.
**Gulf shipping freight rates** have surged as companies reroute cargo via truck to avoid the Strait of Hormuz disruption, adding thousands of dollars per container to import costs.
**Greg Brockman**, OpenAI's co-founder, has returned from leave to take charge of product strategy as the company reportedly plans to merge ChatGPT and its Codex coding product.
**Tommy Robinson** drew tens of thousands to a London rally, calling supporters to prepare for a 'battle of Britain' - turnout was down from his previous march but the rhetoric escalated.
